step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the instructor (UserConsumerDeveloper) triggering CloudFormation, which starts a provisioning flow. This flow includes a Lambda custom resource, which uses Systems Manager to execute scripts on an EC2 instance. This EC2 instance configures AppStream's Image Builder session script and signals back to CloudFormation. For student access, students (UserConsumerWeb) access AppStream via a streaming URL, which connects to EC2 for licensing, mounts FSx for project storage, and connects to an RDS SQL Server Express database.

graph.name: Virtual Software Training with Amazon AppStream 2.0
graph.notes: Automates the provisioning of high-performance GPU virtual training environments for petrotechnical software using CloudFormation, Lambda, and AppStream 2.0.

nodes.name: Instructor
nodes.notes: Initiates deployment of the training class environments.

nodes.name: CloudFormation
nodes.notes: Orchestrates environment resources and custom image creation.

nodes.name: Custom Resource Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggers Systems Manager within execution limits to configure AppStream images.

nodes.name: Systems Manager
nodes.notes: Executes remote configuration scripts on the EC2 instances.

nodes.name: License Server & Script Executor
nodes.notes: Executes image customization scripts and hosts application licensing.

nodes.name: Students
nodes.notes: Access petrotechnical training applications directly via browser streaming URLs.

nodes.name: AppStream 2.0
nodes.notes: Streams GPU-intensive application sessions to student browsers.

nodes.name: FSx for Windows File Server
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores user project files mounted dynamically.

nodes.name: RDS SQL Express
nodes.notes: Database running SQL Server Express to optimize licensing costs.

edges.notes: Remotes into Image Builder to configure session script.
Sends success signal to CloudFormation wait condition.
Deploys final AppStream fleet and stack.
Connects to training application via web browser.
